metadata.methodology: All figures are measured from a real production build and real deployed-system logs. No synthetic/placeholder figures are included.

frontend.measured_against: next build && next start (production server), not dev mode
frontend.note: FID is a real-user (field) metric and cannot be measured by Lighthouse; 'max_potential_FID_ms' is a lab proxy Lighthouse computes, not true FID/INP. No CI/Lighthouse pipeline previously existed in this repo -- this is a fresh, one-off measurement.

backend.measured_against: live production /ws/inference endpoint, champion model P3_SVM (E5/S3/barlow), subject S3
backend.note: The live API has no REST /inference endpoint, no client-selectable model, and no backend TTS step (TTS is client-side via the browser Web Speech API, fired after the response arrives). These are real per-request timings logged by the deployed system, not synthetic requests.

svm_inference_slot1_ms.label: SVM inference (slot 1, incl. Barlow feature extraction)

svm_inference_slot2_ms.label: SVM inference (slot 2, incl. Barlow feature extraction)

weighted_expected_latency.source: SA-syllable proportion in S3's first_syllable_e2e test set (results_S3.json)

theoretical_projection.method: First-order projection: P3 per-model-call latency (T9-cited) x number of P6 model calls.
theoretical_projection.caveat: IMPORTANT -- this projection turned out to be a large underestimate (see real_vs_theoretical_non_sa_case): the T9-cited P3 mean (0.447ms) comes from _measure_p3_latency() in gen_nb_new.py, which times ONLY StandardScaler.transform + SVC.predict on a vector loaded straight from the already feature-extracted Xtest_*.npy -- it never includes Barlow feature extraction from a raw epoch. P6's real measurement (via SubModelBundle.predict_single) DOES extract features from a raw epoch on every call, so the two are not on the same basis. See p3_fair_with_extraction for a same-basis P3 number. Kept here only as a record of the naive projection and why it was wrong.

fair_comparison_same_basis.description: P3 and P6 both timed starting from a raw epoch through feature extraction + scaling + predict (SubModelBundle.predict_single for both) -- unlike p3_champion_latency_cited (T9), which excludes feature extraction entirely. This is the methodologically fair comparison; ratios below are call-count multiples, close to 2x/3x as the theoretical model predicts, confirming feature extraction -- not the cascade structure itself -- was almost the entire cost of the earlier 1300x-looking gap against the T9-cited figure.
